payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new because it matched the requested entities and was the current active record. mem_old was filtered even though it also matched, because it had been superseded by mem_new. In a strict memory system, superseded records may remain in history for traceability but must not be surfaced as the current fact. Punctuation or formatting differences do not change that outcome when both records resolve to the same underlying entities: current active memory is returned, superseded memory is excluded.
payload.decision: Recall selected the newer active memory for Sam associated with Goldman and excluded the older one because the older record was marked superseded by the newer record.

entity_resolution.reason: The explanation concerns existing memory records for the same resolved entities in recall; no new entity creation or merge is required.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new matched the recall query for Sam and Goldman and remained eligible because it is the current, non-superseded memory.
mem_old also matched Sam and Goldman but was filtered because it was superseded, so it should not be presented as current recall.

details: Resolved the explanation as a recall-debug request.
Identified mem_new as selected because it is current and matched the query.
Identified mem_old as excluded because it is superseded.
Preserved historical status without presenting mem_old as current.

repair_options: Verify that mem_old has an explicit superseded status or tombstone marker.
Verify that mem_new carries the replacement link to mem_old.
If both memories are being returned, tighten recall to exclude superseded/deleted records by default.
If entity matching is unstable due to punctuation/normalization, normalize names before ranking and filtering.
If debugging ranking, inspect whether status filtering runs before final result assembly.
